title: Pouteria laetevirens
intro: Pouteria laetevirens, also known as the yellow-leaf pouteria, is a species of flowering plant in the family Sapotaceae. The yellow-leaf pouteria is valued for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial activities, which may contribute to improved cardiovascular health, immune function, and wound healing. The bioactive constituents of this plant include triterpenoids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which have been shown to contribute to its therapeutic effects. The most common herbal preparations made with Pouteria laetevirens are traditional teas and infusions, as well as standardized extracts and capsules, which are used to support overall health and well-being.
taxonomy: Pouteria laetevirens is a species of plant in the family Sapotaceae. It belongs to the genus Pouteria, which consists of about 60 species of trees and shrubs native to tropical and subtropical regions. The family Sapotaceae is a group of flowering plants that are commonly known as sapotes or soapberries. Members of the Sapotaceae family are often characterized by their large, edible fruits, which are rich in oil and have been used in traditional medicine for centuries. Pouteria laetevirens is native to Central and South America, where it grows in a variety of habitats, including tropical forests and dry woodlands.
uses: Pouteria laetevirens is a species of plant that has been used in traditional medicine. Its bark and leaves have been used to treat various ailments, including fever and inflammation. In some cultures, it is believed to have antimicrobial and antifungal properties. The plant's medicinal uses are often associated with its potential to reduce pain and swelling. However, more research is needed to fully understand its medicinal properties and potential applications.
